bead.title: agent: honor triaged operator overrides in readiness
bead.description:
PROBLEM
After an operator reviews a proposed bead and promotes it back to open, pre-claim readiness can evaluate the same finding again and park the bead back to proposed. That creates a downgrade loop and contradicts the operator intent to keep work moving.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md:70-75 already defines proposed -> open as operator-driven triaged, but cli/internal/bead/store.go:587-628 status transitions mutate lifecycle metadata without necessarily appending a durable triaged event that readiness can query.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1207-1226 unconditionally parks terminal readiness findings without checking for a later operator triage/acceptance.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2784-2815 stores intake.blocked without a stable fingerprint, so even if an operator override exists the loop cannot tell whether the current readiness finding is the same one.

PROPOSED FIX
- Reuse the existing TD-027 triaged semantics for proposed -> open. Ensure operator promotion records a durable signal: either a triaged event or an explicit operator-accepted marker attached to the status transition event body.
- Add a stable readiness finding fingerprint for parkable intake findings. Include at least the decision class/rule, normalized reason, and a hash of prompt-relevant bead fields used to determine whether the bead changed since operator acceptance.
- Before parking an open bead for pre-claim readiness, check whether a later proposed -> open operator acceptance covers the same fingerprint. If yes, append/emit override-honored evidence and proceed instead of parking.
- If prompt-relevant fields changed after the operator acceptance, allow readiness to re-evaluate and park with a new fingerprint.

NON-SCOPE
- No broad WARN/BLOCK policy changes; those are in ddx-91898f19.
- No new event vocabulary unless the spec/observability bead authorizes it.
- No changes to closed/reopen semantics for closed -> open.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. Depends on ddx-ef9e8aec.
bead.acceptance:
1. Add or update TestLifecycleProposedToOpenAppendsTriaged to prove operator status update proposed -> open leaves durable triaged/operator-acceptance evidence visible to the readiness loop.
2. Add TestPreClaimIntakeOperatorOverridePreventsRepeatParking: seed a bead that was parked for readiness, promote it proposed -> open, run the same readiness finding again, and assert status remains open or proceeds to claim instead of proposed.
3. Add TestPreClaimIntakeChangedBeadInvalidatesOverride: after the operator promotion, mutate prompt-relevant bead text/AC and prove a new hard operator_required finding may park with a new fingerprint.
4. Production wiring is covered: the override check runs before every pre-claim ParkToProposed call in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go, not only in a test helper.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(LifecycleProposedToOpenAppendsTriaged|PreClaimIntakeOperatorOverridePreventsRepeatParking|PreClaimIntakeChangedBeadInvalidatesOverride)"` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

{"detail":"This is not atomic. TD-031 §7.2 says proposed-\u003eopen triage is the operator-acceptance signal for readiness idempotency, but the repo has multiple acceptance paths (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:205-249` via `BeadReopen`/`BeadApprove`, plus `Store.Reopen` in `cli/internal/bead/store.go:1372-1410`) while the repeat-parking bug lives in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2780-2815`. The bead also mixes two independently testable changes: durable acceptance-marker plumbing and fingerprint-based suppression of repeat parking after the same finding. Those should be split before claim.","intake_outcome":"too_large_decomposed"}

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"description drops commitment \"cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2784\"","fingerprint":"9a9f3e7563c088c6559b50170a909615619d5996d3af04d242043c3656f19c85","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"rewrite_rejected","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.rewrite_rejected","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}

{"rationale":"Strong bead overall: title, root-cause-grounded description, numbered verification with named tests and commands, labels, and parent/deps all satisfy the lint rubric. The only miss is sufficiency as a single sub-agent prompt: it bundles two independently testable implementation tracks, durable operator-acceptance plumbing and repeat-parking fingerprint suppression, so it is slightly overloaded for one execution bead.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["Split the durable acceptance-marker plumbing from the repeat-parking fingerprint suppression into separate child beads, or narrow this bead to one implementation path with the other as a dependency.","If kept as one bead, explicitly constrain file ownership and sequence so a single agent can implement and verify without needing scope clarification."],"waivers_applied":[]}

metadata.needs-human-reason: This is not atomic. TD-031 §7.2 says proposed->open triage is the operator-acceptance signal for readiness idempotency, but the repo has multiple acceptance paths (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:205-249` via `BeadReopen`/`BeadApprove`, plus `Store.Reopen` in `cli/internal/bead/store.go:1372-1410`) while the repeat-parking bug lives in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2780-2815`. Those should be split before claim.
